Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req. For Admissions (Set One) And Form Interrogatories (Set Two) Req. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
No appearance stated for Plaintiff/movng party
Pamela B. Shafer, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff MARSHA CHISHOLM's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions (Set One) and Form Interrogatory (Set Two) No. 17.1 is moot as further responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. However, Defendant is advised that the court would not approve the objections asserted in those supplemental responses. Defendant is also advised that the manner in which he has responded to certain Requests, using the phrase "in recent times," is not responsive. The parties shall meet and confer to resolve any remaining disputes about the supplemental responses.
The court finds that the inadequate responses and late supplemental responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defense Counsel in the amount of $1,250, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
